steps.title: Track the pivot
steps.content: The first two sentences divide Native languages into families and isolates. Isolates are described as having no demonstrable genealogical relationship to other languages today. The underlined sentence, introduced by 'Yet,' says isolates such as Washoe and Chitimacha may be remnants of older families whose other members disappeared before the record preserved them.

steps.title: Explain the function
steps.content: A invents influence from another language. B reverses the disappearance claim by saying isolate ancestors replaced nonisolate languages. C focuses on record bias rather than vanished relatives. The underlined sentence instead suggests that languages classified as isolates now may once have belonged to families, so today's isolate/nonisolate distinction may not always have applied in the distant past. The answer is D.
